title: $1,000 in Apple on iPhone Launch Day (2007)
description: What $1,000 invested in Apple on January 9, 2007 — the day Steve Jobs unveiled the first iPhone — would be worth today with dividends reinvested. The iPhone announcement was the inflection point that transformed Apple from a computer maker into the most valuable consumer company on Earth.
dividend_note: Apple reinstated its dividend in August 2012 at $0.38/share/quarter. DRIP assumed from first payment. Dividend has grown every year since.
educational_note: Apple's two splits (7:1 in 2014, 4:1 in 2020) turned 83 shares into over 2,300. Dividends reinvested from 2012 onward meaningfully added to total return. Apple also went through a painful correction in 2008-2009 (down ~60%) that an investor had to survive.
